properties.text:
These Slack logs record several on‑call handoffs, incident mitigations, and a route‑lifecycle design proposal across different Redwood services.
- Alice Nguyen started a private on‑prem rollout of v3.4.0 to oak‑2 with deploybot autosuppress; DeployBot saw latency rise to 520 ms and error‑rate 0.8 % but auto‑rollback was blocked, and Alice later pinned the deployment to v3.3.9.
- Ben Carter reported a 5xx spike on oak‑2‑app‑1 during a high‑IO backup (node‑3 at 95 % disk usage), prompting manual mitigation.
- Alex Cheng on‑call observed p99 2.8 s and ~12 k queue depth in us‑west‑2; Priya Natarajan traced the issue to warm‑up slot starvation with 9 pending warm‑ups.
- Miguel Torres noted 12‑second handshake timeouts for the redwood‑quant‑v2 model; Samir Patel raised desired replicas to 30 and disabled warmup_preload, dropping p95 to 420 ms and p99 to 1.1 s.
- Kernel rollbacks on nodes 47 and 49 cut pending warm‑ups to 1 and lowered p99 to ≈750 ms; Priya Natarajan took post‑mortem ownership (24 h ETA).
- Nora Blake proposed an “intent‑first” route‑lifecycle contract with fields like `intent_expiry_seconds` and `soft_deprecate_seconds`; the team added signed intent bundles, a clamp/audit log, a prune‑plan workflow, telemetry events, and a preview API/CLI.
- An OpsBot epic RW‑PLT‑9001 was opened to implement the contract, rollout via feature flag `route-intent-lifecycle-v2`, and create docs and test harnesses.
- In eu‑west‑1, embedding latency spiked (p95 120 ms→520 ms, 429 rate up); Ava Chen took primary, Ravi Patel increased HPA minReplicas to 8, and a temporary routing rule to `model‑emb‑small` brought p95 down to 210 ms and 429 to 0.9 %.
- Maya Chen handed off her shift to Ethan Park due to a flight delay; Ethan assumed primary for a gen.prod us‑east‑1 latency spike, applied prefix‑cache warm‑up, kept gn‑large canaries, and prepared a fallback to `gn‑medium` if p95 exceeded 1.3 s.
- Priya Singh noted autoscale burst settings differ by region and monitored queue depth; Liam O’Connor stood by as L2 support.
- Maya Chen logged two affected customers (C‑312, C‑721) and informed their CSMs; the incident was resolved after latency fell to ~1.5 s.
- The on‑call handoff was recorded, with appropriate updates to the on‑call sheet and pager acknowledgments.

Actor: ['ethan-park']
Occurred at: 8160000450000
Content:
#eng-oncall: 8160000000

Maya Chen: hey team — unexpected flight change, stuck at SFO. my primary window ends in ~2.5h. can someone take primary for the last 2h? i can stay on shadow until wheels up if needed :airplane:
Ethan Park: @Maya Chen yep i can take the end of your shift. do you need me to run through any hot contexts?
Maya Chen: thanks so much. main things to watch: 1) embed pipeline errors (we saw a retry blip earlier), 2) latency spike on gen.prod.us-east-1 for large prompts. last incident had fallback to gn-medium which helped.
Maya Chen: runbook refs: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/prim-sec-handshake and the incident checklist: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/latency-spike-checklist
Aria (oncall-bot): Incident bot: new pager created — INC-9241 — gen.prod latency spike (p95 -> 1.9s). Pager pushed to primary Maya Chen, secondary Ethan Park. Pager link: https://pager.redwood/INC-9241
pagerbot: Pager: INC-9241 acknowledged by Maya Chen at 8160000060
Priya Singh: @Ethan Park if you're taking primary, quick note: autoscale policy for gen.prod has burst disabled in eu region; US has burst enabled. for this window, fallback policy is to route to gn-medium in us-east.
Ethan Park: got it. Maya, can you hand over current mitigation steps? any open canaries or ongoing rollouts?
Maya Chen: nothing rolling now. mitigation in place: prefix cache warm, 2 canaries of gn-large pinned to 1.4. we had a temporary KV cache miss—reconciled earlier but watch for a reappearance. i put a short snippet in the runbook:
```
# quick check list
curl -sS https://telemetry.redwood/api/latency?env=prod | jq .p95
redwoodctl infra scale --pool gen-prod --to 6
# rollback canary if p95 > 2.0 for >5m
```
Liam O'Connor: copying runbook step: if p95 > 2.2 and retries spike, openthrottle patch: `kubectl -n infra set env deploy/gen-proxy MAX_PENDING=200` and notify infra.
Ethan Park: i'll take primary at 8160000100. Maya, can you confirm you've handed over slack/channel privileges and updated oncall sheet?
Maya Chen: done — edited the sheet, added "Maya -> Ethan 2h handoff (flight)". also added short notes under the thread with the KV key prefixes to watch.
Ethan Park: ok taking primary. quick contact plan: if anything escalates I'll ping #eng-sre and @Priya Singh for autoscale help. Liam, can you be standby L2 for the next 45m?
Liam O'Connor: yep i can standby for 45m then i've got a meeting. i'll stay reachable for urgent pager escalation.
Priya Singh: i'll monitor autoscale and queue depths from my side. if we need to flip to gn-medium, i'll coordinate the rollout and update the console.
Aria (oncall-bot): Handoff recorded: Maya Chen -> Ethan Park; start 8160000100; expected end 81600008200. Note: please manually confirm when Maya disconnects.
Maya Chen: quick extra: customer impact — 2 customers (id: C-312, C-721) saw degraded perf earlier. i've emailed their CSMs and flagged for postmortem if we hit >5% error rate again.
Ethan Park: thanks — i'll add those customers to the post-incident note if we see regressions. taking pager into rotation now.
pagerbot: Pager: INC-9241 acknowledged by Ethan Park at 8160000110
Ethan Park: initial checks — p95 trending down to 1.5s after a short autoscale; watch for regressions. i'm going to keep the canary at gn-large for now for 15m.
Liam O'Connor: FYI ran `redwoodctl monitor --pool gen-prod --metric queue_depth` — no sustained growth.
Priya Singh: thumbs — i'll prepare the gn-medium fallback command in case. putting snippet here:
```
# fallback to gn-medium
redwoodctl model route set gen-prod --variant gn-medium --weight 100 --dry-run=false
```
Ethan Park: if p95 drops below 1.3 and stable for 10m i'll mark incident resolved and hand back to Maya after she checks in. Maya, safe travels. ping me if you want a final quick sync before gate. :thumbsup:
Maya Chen: cheers everyone — will message once wheels up. appreciate the quick pick up. :wave:

Source event id: slack:7167800000
Source app: slack
Source path: sources/slack/incidents/7167800000-shift-snapshot-midday-coverage-sync.json
Actor: ['ava-chen']
Occurred at: 7167865400000
Content:
#eng-oncall: 7167800000

Marta (incident-bot): ⚠️ Incident summary for shift handoff — embeddings routing spikes seen in eu-west-1. Root page at 7167789000. Paging noisy but customer impact appears limited to ~4–6% of embedding requests. See brief below and actions taken.

Ava Chen: taking primary for the next 8h. I’m on call and will own escalations. Ravi — you ok to be secondary?

Ravi Patel: secondary, available. ping me if pages escalate. quick question: is this still only embeddings?

Diego Morales: yep, embeddings pipeline only. Latency p95 up from 120ms -> 520ms, and 429 rate increased in the egress router. CPU on the embedding workers trending high.

Jules - SRE: noticed autoscaler oscillating on embedding-deployment-3. Throttle / eviction logs show a tight loop:
```
2026-03-15T11:02:03Z embedding-worker[pid=1423]: eviction: cache_pressure=0.92
2026-03-15T11:02:03Z router: 429 respond - queue_len=410
```

Ava Chen: thanks. Current status: impact = partial degradation, affects embedding endpoints in eu-west-1 only, about 4–6% of requests either 429 or >500ms. No active customer escalations yet.

Ewa (oncall-helper): runbook refs: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/embeddings-autoscaler and https://internal.redwood/playbooks/egress-429. Starting checklist applied: scale up replica count, bump minReplicas=4 -> 8, pause autoscaler ramp-down.

Ravi Patel: applied minReplicas change via k8s patch. Command used:
```kubectl -n embeddings patch hpa embedding-deployment-3 -p '{"spec":{"minReplicas":8}}'```
responded OK.

Diego Morales: I put a temporary routing rule to divert low-priority embedding requests to model-emb-small (lower-cost, lower-latency) to reduce pressure. Rule live: route: embeddings.eu-west-1 -> prefer model-emb-large unless queue_len>300 then fallback to model-emb-small.

Ava Chen: good. Any signs of backend OOMs or node flaps?

Jules - SRE: no OOMs, but one node reported high swap usage. I cordoned node euw-gpu-12 to avoid further churn and drained pods to spread load.

Marta (incident-bot): Pager resolution timeline: page started 11:01 UTC; mitigations started 11:15 UTC; partial service relief 11:24 UTC.

Ewa (oncall-helper): I also enabled prefix KV cache warmup for the most common embedding keys to reduce recompute. This is a config toggle in feature-flags: embeddings.prefetch=true.

Ravi Patel: monitoring dashboards updated: embedding-p95 now 210ms, 429 rate down to 0.9% in eu-west-1. Still above baseline but improving.

Ava Chen: next steps for whoever picks this up later: 1) keep autoscaler minReplicas=8 for 2 hours, 2) watch for oscillation, 3) run heap/profile on embedding-worker-# if CPU stays high, 4) if 429s creep back above 2%, consider temporarily enabling global fallback to model-emb-small for all eu-west traffic.

Diego Morales: note for postmortem: autoscaler tuning keyset causes oscillation when cache-miss rate spikes. Dev-ml team to investigate quantization/prepare smaller model variant to reduce cold compute.

Jules - SRE: adding runbook pointer in the thread for bridge and follow-up: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/embeddings-postmortem-guidelines. Also leaving a TODO to improve alert thresholds to reduce noisy pages.

Ava Chen: handoff specifics: primary = Ava Chen, secondary = Ravi Patel. On-call bridge is open until 12:30 UTC for coverage if anyone needs synchronous sync. Current state = degraded -> recovering. Customer impact = limited, no major customers reported.

Ewa (oncall-helper): final checks I ran:
- Confirmed k8s patch applied and pods created (embedding-worker-8/8 up)
- Confirmed routing rule via feature-flags is active
- Verified no increase in errors in downstream fallback metrics

Ravi Patel: ack. I’ll stay on standby and can take over if Ava steps out. If you need an exec note prepared, I can draft a short update for support.

Ava Chen: thanks. If things look stable in 30m we’ll relax minReplicas to 6 and monitor for 1h. Leaving these action items in the thread:
- [Ava] monitor p95 and 429, adjust minReplicas down only if stable for 30m
- [Ravi] prepare customer comms draft in case escalations appear
- [Diego] follow up with ML infra on quantization/worker optimizations
- [Jules] schedule post-incident scrub for next oncall rotation

Marta (incident-bot): Incident closed to critical state at 11:40 UTC — still in monitoring. Page count: 7. Reopen if full regression. :white_check_mark:

Ava Chen: handing over context to next shift. If there’s regression, page primary/secondary and shout in #eng-oncall. handing over — Ava out. :wave:

Ravi Patel: taking notes, will pick up if needed. good handoff. :thumbsup:

Diego Morales: leaving logs and traces snippet here for later debug:
```
trace_id=abc123 span=encoder-latency  duration=480ms
sample_keys=[user:123, doc:kg-45, prompt:short]
```

Jules - SRE: added internal timeline and tag "embeddings-eu-west-03" to the incident tracker.

Ewa (oncall-helper): thanks all. Closing this handover thread.

Oncall Bot: Reminder — next on-call shift starts 12:00 UTC, please follow the pre-shift checklist: https://internal.redwood/checklists/oncall-preflight

Source event id: slack:7088881234
Source app: slack
Source path: sources/slack/architecture/7088881234-intent-first-route-lifecycle-contract.json
Actor: ['ethan-park']
Occurred at: 7088884567000
Content:
#architecture: 7088881234

Nora Blake: Quick sync — I want to propose a compact contract for route lifecycle that reduces ambiguity between policy authors and the runtime. Too many incidents from routes disappearing.
Ethan Park: +1. High level: policy expresses intent; runtime enforces safety. We need explicit fields and a clear clamp/audit mechanism.
Maya Torres: Suggested minimal data model: `intent_expiry_seconds` (nullable), `soft_deprecate_seconds`, `prune_after_seconds`. Runtime exposes `safety_cap_seconds` per deployment and may shorten any intent but must log `consensus_clamp` with `actor` and `reason`.
Sven Müller: For private/on-prem installs add signed intent bundles. Control plane signs; local admin can sign for air-gapped installs. Runtime validates signature and rejects expired bundles.
Nora Blake: Coordinator question: who schedules prunes? Control plane preferred for hosted customers; local runtime for local-only customers. We should support both but keep canonical precedence: control-plane > local.
Ethan Park: Implementation detail: when control-plane is present it publishes a `prune-plan` in batches. Runtimes can `dry-run` the plan and report back before enforcement. Dry-run mode also helps private customers validate.
Maya Torres: Telemetry contract: events `route.intent_declared`, `route.intent_clamped`, `route.soft_deprecated`, `route.pruned`. Fields: policy_id, route_id, declared_ttl, effective_ttl, clamp_reason, runtime_version, deployment_mode. Keep payload small.
Sven Müller: API ergonomics: add `GET /v1/policies/{id}/prune-preview?window=72h` and CLI `redwood route preview --policy-id`. Also runtime should surface header `X-Redwood-Route-Deprecation: soft;eta=2026-03-12T12:00:00Z` during soft-deprecate window.
Nora Blake: Linting rules: reject `ttl: 0` unless `permanent: true`. Warn when legacy `ttl` used; require new policies to include `intent_*` fields. Add auto-fix suggestion in CI.
Ethan Park: Emergency ops: `operator_override` endpoint that is timeboxed (auto-expire 1h) and requires an attached post-mortem ticket. Overrides must appear in telemetry as `operator_override:<id>`.
Maya Torres: Migration plan: 1) accept both legacy `ttl` and new `intent_*` fields and emit warnings; 2) enable linter enforcement for PRs; 3) after grace period start enforcing clamps only on new routes, then migrate existing routes via console-assisted flow.
Sven Müller: Testing: add `route-lifecycle-sim` harness to exercise active->soft->prune transitions, signed-bundle verification tests, and clamp audit propagation. Include integration test that validates header emission and audit events.
Nora Blake: Rollout: feature-flag `route-intent-lifecycle-v2`. Start internal, then opt-in beta, then default. Provide a clear opt-out for enterprise/private customers.
Ethan Park: Docs checklist: data model (examples), CLI commands, migration steps, operator runbook for overrides, and signed-bundle key rotation guide for private customers.
Maya Torres: Action items: I'll draft the schema + linter PR. Sven prototype telemetry and signed-bundle flow. Ethan draft preview API + CLI. OpsBot: create epic for rollout.
OpsBot: Epic created RW-PLT-9001 "Intent-first route lifecycle & prune plan". Assigned owners and initial milestones.
Nora Blake: Revisit in 4 working days after PRs land. Thanks everyone. :thumbsup:

Source event id: slack:6865432109
Source app: slack
Source path: sources/slack/incidents/6865432109-autoscale-warmup-west-coast-throttle-triage.json
Actor: ['priya-natarajan']
Occurred at: 6865433909000
Content:
#incidents: 6865432109

incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident 6865432109 - Dedicated pool us-west-2 experiencing p99 latency spike (2.8s) and streaming stalls. Autoscaler warmup delays detected. Paging oncall + eng-sre. Dash: https://dashboards.redwood/incident/6865432109

Alex Cheng: ack, I'm on call. seeing spike on the graphs now. p95 ~800ms, p99 ~2.8s, backlog queue depth jumped to ~12k. :grimacing:

Jordan Wells: we've got 3 paying tenants reporting degraded streaming (long stalls then reconnect). CS notified. Trying to gather which customers exactly now.

Priya Natarajan: logging in to control plane now. first pass: what changed in last deploy? any model variant rollouts or kernel changes?

Miguel Torres: nothing rolling in prod in last 2 hours AFAIK. runtime telemetry shows a surge of warmup failures, handshake times spiking. suspect warmup slot starvation in us-west pool.

Samir Patel: checking autoscaler logs and pool state. pulling current desired vs running:```json
{"pool":"us-west-2-dedicated","desired_replicas":24,"ready_replicas":18,"warming_replicas":3,"pending_warmups":9,"queue_depth":12134}```

Priya Natarajan: pending_warmups 9 while ready < desired -> autoscaler triggered but warmup latency high. Any kv-cache coldpath or heavy quantized loads?

Miguel Torres: runtime logs show repeated handshake timeouts (~12s) for warmup tasks. excerpt:```
[2026-03-15T10:12:03Z] warmup: slot_alloc failed handshake_timeout_ms=12000 model_variant=redwood-quant-v2
[2026-03-15T10:12:04Z] warmup: retrying slot_alloc
```

Alex Cheng: could be kernel selection regression on the new runtime build. Sam, can you check node GPU load and if any nodes stuck in init state?

Samir Patel: running node check:```
redwoodctl nodes list --pool us-west-2-dedicated
# output (trimmed)
node-42 gpu_util=98% status=ready
node-43 gpu_util=95% status=ready
node-47 gpu_util=3%  status=initializing (warmup hung)
node-49 gpu_util=2%  status=initializing (warmup hung)
```
Looks like 2 nodes stuck initializing, not accepting warmups. Investigating kube events.

Leah Kim: customers on the hotshot tier will notice first. Can we route their traffic to a fallback region or a smaller model variant temporarily?

Alex Cheng: immediate mitigations: 1) scale reserve capacity up (manual), 2) enable fallback to eu-west for non-data-residency tenants, 3) reduce concurrency for streaming to shed load. I'm going to manually bump pool desired to 30 to force more provisioning.

Priya Natarajan: manual bump ok, but warmup still needs to finish. Suggest we also toggle warmup_preload=false for a minute so we don't block on full model load for some requests. That would trade latency for correctness but reduce handshake pressure.

Miguel Torres: agree. toggling warmup_preload for hotshot routing paths. issuing command:```bash
redwoodctl config set --pool us-west-2-dedicated warmup_preload=false --reason "live mitigation 6865432109"
```

Samir Patel: bumped desired_replicas to 30. provisioning started. kube events show nodes spinning up, but a lot depends on image pull and kernel module init times (we're seeing a slow init on a subset).

Jordan Wells: notifying affected customers: "We're investigating increased streaming latency in US-West. We've routed traffic to fallback capacity and are working on mitigation. Will update in 15m."

incident-bot: Notification: oncall escalation to eng-sre@redwood for p99 breach. Runbook link: https://runbooks.redwood/pools/warmup-starvation

Alex Cheng: quick test of failover routing: I toggled a small percentage of traffic to eu-west and p99 for that shard dropped to 580ms. Good signal.

Priya Natarajan: current telemetry after mitigations: p95=420ms, p99=1.1s, queue_depth=4.2k, pending_warmups=3. Improvement but still above SLO at p99.

Miguel Torres: starting to roll a temporary kernel fallback on 4 nodes (previous stable kernel) to see if handshake timeouts stop. Will coordinate with infra.

Samir Patel: kernel rollback staged to node-47,node-49,node-51,node-52. Applying one-by-one to avoid mass restart. If rollback clears init hang, warmup should catch up.

Leah Kim: product wants a customer impact summary when we have a stable snapshot. Also flagging this for the weekly reliability review.

Alex Cheng: status update: kernel rollback completed on node-47 and node-49. warmup failures for those nodes dropped, pending_warmups->1, queue_depth->1800. p99 trending toward 750ms.

Jordan Wells: CS: two customers reported timeouts earlier, both on hotshot; we've confirmed retries for streaming succeeded after failover. No financial data impacted.

Priya Natarajan: next steps: 1) keep fallback routing until pool warmups finish, 2) collect warmup handshake traces and attach to incident, 3) run postmortem to determine why image init/kmod path regressed.

Miguel Torres: grabbing traces now, will upload to the incident timeline. Also scheduling a canary roll of the new runtime build into a small pool to reproduce locally.

Alex Cheng: handing over to eng-sre for post-incident follow-up. If anything regresses ping this thread. Thanks all. :heartpulse:

Leah Kim: noted — product will draft comms for impacted customers and a short status update for slack channels once postmortem is ready.

incident-bot: Incident 6865432109 - mitigation in progress. Current severity: Sev2. Open tasks: attach traces, postmortem owner, update runbook with warmup rollback play.

Alex Cheng: postmortem owner: Priya. I'll close paging once p99 < 1s for 15m sustained.

Priya Natarajan: taking postmortem ownership. ETA for initial writeup 24h. Closing updates for now — leaving thread open for any followups.

---

Source event id: slack:6472105432
Source app: slack
Source path: sources/slack/eng-infra/6472105432-tempstore-io-latency-spike-post-rolling-restart.json
Actor: ['ava-chen']
Occurred at: 6472105750000
Content:
#eng-infra: 6472105432

Micah Nguyen: heads-up — after the 03:30 rolling restart window I saw a tempstore IO latency spike in us-west1, GPU attaches started failing with attach timeouts and a bunch of pending pods. Stacktrace-ish logs attached below. Anyone seen this pattern?

Ava Chen: :eyes: can you paste the iostat / dstat snippet? also which janitor/cleaner cron ran?

Micah Nguyen: iostat 1 5 showed crazy await on nvme0n1p1:
```
Device:            tps    kB_read/s    kB_wrtn/s    kB_read    kB_wrtn
nvme0n1           50.0       1200.0       9800.0       6000      49000
```
And kube attach logs: `AttachVolume timed out after 120s waiting for device` — lots of tempfile cleanup activity in syslog. Cron job `janitor-sweep` started 03:32 on many nodes (we rolled 03:25-03:40).

Dmitri Petrov: rolling and janitor overlapped — we had the janitor schedule at */5 during the deploy patch, my bad. It forks up to 12 workers by default. Config snippet: `JOBS=12` in /etc/janitor/config.

Marta Lopez: that explains it. The janitor does full readdir + unlink loops on /tmp/scratch, which blows out small-block IOPS. We should throttle it during cluster restarts. Suggestion: set MAX_WORKERS=2 and add backoff.

Ryan O'Connor: Agree. Also saw provisioner queues back up; autoscaler then requested more Dedicated GPUs to compensate for timeouts, so cost blip. We need a short mitigation to stop autoscaler panic.

Micah Nguyen: mitigation plan proposal:
- temp: scale down janitor workers to 1 on impacted nodes (`sed -i 's/JOBS=12/JOBS=1/' /etc/janitor/config && systemctl restart janitor`)
- patch: add a lockfile so janitor skips if node recently restarted (`/var/run/node-reboot.lock`)
- infra: add Prometheus alert for sustained nvme await > 50ms for >2m to gate autoscaler triggers
- ops: rollback only if attach errors > 10% for 5m

Ben Shah: I can roll the sed+restart across the affected ASG now. Which tag set?

Micah Nguyen: tag = fleet:gpu-temp1, region=us-west1, pool=scratch-high. Start with 10 nodes and monitor.

Ava Chen: quick Prometheus query to watch: `avg_over_time(node_disk_io_time_seconds_total{device="nvme0n1"}[2m]) * 1000` — plot that and hook to alert. Also setting autoscaler cooldown to 8m would reduce flapping.

Dmitri Petrov: I'll change janitor config repo to default MAX_WORKERS=2 and add a heartbeat file check. Will open PR and backport patch to current deploys.

infra-bot: alert: nvme_await_high_us-west1 - 03:34 - firing on 12 nodes. links: https://grafana/redwood/d/abcd/nvme-io?from=...

Marta Lopez: long-term: we should move small-file GC off-host into a queue/worker pool that respects node IO pressure. Also consider async unlink or batching unlinks to reduce metadata churn.

Ryan O'Connor: let's also add a temporary autoscaler suppression label `suppress-autoscale: temp-io` that prevents scale-ups for 10m when set by ops. We can use it as a circuit-breaker while janitor repairs land.

Micah Nguyen: accepted. Ben please run the restart change, Dmitri open the PR, Ava add the Prom alert + extend autoscaler cooldown, Marta create design issue for off-host GC, Ryan we'll add suppression label. Update here when done.

Ben Shah: running now on 10 nodes — patch applied to fleet:gpu-temp1. `for n in $(aws --region us-west1 ec2 describe-instances --filters "Name=tag:fleet,Values=gpu-temp1" ...); do ssh $n 'sed -i "s/JOBS=12/JOBS=1/" /etc/janitor/config && systemctl restart janitor'; done`

Micah Nguyen: thx — seeing attach retries clearing up, nvme await trending down. Will leave CR on for 30m, then revert if stable. :thumbsup:

Ava Chen: PR for alert created: https://git.redwood/ops/alerts/pull/72 — query + 2m for firing.

Dmitri Petrov: PR for janitor config change: https://git.redwood/infra/janitor/pull/518 — includes lockfile logic.

Marta Lopez: created design issue: https://git.redwood/infra/issues/1342 - off-host GC draft.

Micah Nguyen: marking this as resolved for now, will watch through the rest of the rolling restart window. Thanks all. :rocket:

Content:
#product: 6378901234

Priya Desai: Quick sync — we have a customer asking for an end-to-end pilot of FastRoute in ~2 weeks. Main blockers: infra prewarm (reserved capacity), runtime quantization/profile, and KMS/HSM key flow. I think we should decide staged scope today. TL;DR: staging-only pilot first, HSM later. Thoughts?
Alex Chen: Staging-only sounds safest. Runtime can cut a quantization profile and a conservative batching default, but 2 weeks is tight. Can we limit telemetry and non-blocking features? :thinking_face:
Samir Patel: Capacity note: to guarantee latency SLOs we need to reserve a small GPU pool and prewarm it for the pilot window. That requires a capacity request and cost sign-off. 2 weeks -> we need the request by EOD to make it.
Jordan Blake: Security: HSM/KMS review + customer key ownership is non-trivial. We can run without HSM in staging if the customer accepts envelope keys, but production (or anything with BYOK) needs the KMS review and attestation.
Riley Gomez: Customer preference is end-to-end with keys, but they said they'd accept staging-only to validate latency and UX. Product priority is verifying p95 latency and core correctness, not BYOK yet.
Alex Chen: Proposed rollout plan (small):
```
rollout:
  stage1: staging-only (no HSM), canary 10% internal, reduced telemetry
  stage2: HSM-enabled (BYOK) after KMS review, full telemetry
runtime_configs:
  default_batch: 32
  model_variant: quantized-v2
  fallback: fp16-v1
```
Samir Patel: Ok — if we do staging-only, infra needs a pinned pool for the pilot window and a jittered prewarm schedule. I'll file the capacity request. @Priya please confirm expected pilot traffic shape (qps, concurrency).
Priya Desai: Expected pilot traffic: ~150 qps peak, concurrency spike to ~60. We'll provide a small synthetic trace. Priorities: p95 < 250ms, correct fallbacks.
Jordan Blake: I'll kick off the KMS/HSM intake with security docs but it will slip past the 2-week pilot unless we deprioritize other reviews.
Alex Chen: For rollout safety, add this fallback rule: `if gpu_latency > 500ms -> route to fp16-v1`. Also enable KV prefix caching for common prompts. That helps p95.
deploy-bot: scheduled prewarm job created: `prewarm-fastroute-637890` -> starts in 36h (automated).
Riley Gomez: Decision call: staging-only pilot in 2w with reserved capacity + reduced telemetry. HSM/BYOK moved to next cycle (Q3). Action items: @Samir file capacity request by EOD, @Jordan start KMS intake, @Alex ship quant profile + fallback, @Priya send traffic trace. Agree? :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: Filing capacity request now.
Jordan Blake: Starting KMS intake, will post checklist.
Alex Chen: I'll drop quant profile PR today and add the fallback rule.
Priya Desai: Sending traffic trace in #product-docs in 15m.
Riley Gomez: Great — locking staging-first. We can reconvene mid-week if anything blocks. Thanks everyone. :rocket:

properties.text:
The logs capture several Slack communications covering a compact data‑privacy pilot, Q2 OKR status updates, a new hire onboarding process, a partial production rollback, admission budgeting proposals, documentation link‑sweeping, SLA exception planning for regulated pilots, and release‑notes preparation.
- Ben Carter and teammates defined a compact DP validation pilot called “dp‑priv‑mini‑2025‑11” targeting six partners with 3‑week staggered starts and 21‑45 day pilots, including performance SLOs, legal addendum, incentives, and tooling.
- Ivy Cho specified latency SLOs for generation (p50 < 80 ms, p95 < 300 ms at 256 tokens) and embeddings (p50 < 30 ms, p95 < 80 ms) with separate buckets.
- Samir Khan listed success gates for the pilot: integration tests, performance SLOs, six labeled failure cases, and partner feedback cadence.
- Asha Reddy offered incentives of two months discounted Dedicated credits, up to three engineering days, and co‑marketing for strategic partners.
- dp‑bot announced the cohort creation and that invitations were queued for six prospects, with kick‑offs planned for the week of 2025‑11‑17.
- Jordan Blake coordinated Q2 OKR flightcheck, requesting one‑line status updates on shipped or slipped components, blocking dependencies, and PR reviews.
- Maria Gonzales reported that runtime‑v3 autoscale Canary at 50 % was shipped, awaiting a PR review and a GPU image upgrade (ticket #PLAT‑87).
- Chen Li noted an e2e test flake on Dedicated, a blocking infra dependency on a new GPU image, and a downstream dependency map for the autoscale rollout.
- Samir Patel indicated the console telemetry retention feature slipped due to storage budget overflow, proposing a toggle and back‑fill job, and referenced PR #88 needing review.
- Deploy‑bot scheduled a canary deployment of runtime‑v3 autoscale for 2026‑03‑22T18:00Z.
- Haris Malik introduced himself as a new Developer Experience hire, asked about MDM enrollment, VPN, and onboarding buddy, and later reported successful MDM enrollment and SSH jump‑host access.
- Onboard‑bot created Haris’s account, provided checklist links, and confirmed completion of enrollment steps.
- Ava Chen acted as Haris’s onboarding buddy, sending calendar invites and VPN configuration.
- Marta Li (IT) guided Haris through MDM enrollment and hardware ticket creation, and helped resolve a certificate error.
- Sam Wells set up jump‑host SSH access for Haris and applied the ACL.
- Priya Nair presented two admission‑budgeting proposals (per‑tenant queues vs shared backlog with budget hints) and shared a JSON budget_hint schema.
- Ethan Cole supported evaluating budget hints and suggested a `prefetch_ok` flag for I/O‑heavy prompts.
- Samir Gupta raised concerns about memory blow‑up with per‑tenant queues and favored shared backlog with soft budget hints.
- Marta Lopez proposed ephemeral per‑tenant handles with a compact index into the shared backlog.
- Ava Chen agreed on an admission ticket API returning lease, credits, and window estimates, emphasizing read‑only consumption by the coalescer.
- The team assigned experiment and implementation tasks: Ethan to create a harness, Samir to add budget_hint fields, Marta to prototype handle lifecycle, and Ava to draft the ticket API.
- Deploy‑bot queued a canary deployment of the batcher‑v2‑experiment to staging.
- Noah Kim reported six orphan or broken KB links identified by the link‑sweeper and asked for owners.
- Asha Rao claimed two pages (serving‑limits and gpu‑quotas), planned redirects, re‑hosting a broken image, and added a localization label.
- Ethan Park requested commits to include original Git SHA and a `localize:check‑ja` label for onboarding changes.
- A PR #12950 was opened to fix the orphan links, add redirects, rehost images, and include changelog entries; it passed CI and was merged.
- Nina Park raised a request for SLA exceptions for two regulated pilot customers, prompting a coordination plan among Raj Singh, Omar Reyes, Lena Mfcller, Ben Carter, and Emma Zhao.
- Raj Singh outlined legal conditions for SLA exceptions, Omar Reyes listed required security controls, Lena noted customers’ willingness to sign addendums and pay for higher SLA credits, Ben estimated a 30 % ops headcount cost increase, and Emma prepared a billing backstop clause.
- The team scheduled drafting of the exception addendum, security gap check, billing terms, and sales finalization between Tuesday and Thursday, agreeing to keep the exceptions private/pilot‑only.
- Ava Chen posted a release‑notes/changelog draft (PR 904) and asked volunteers to add a migration blurb for embed‑streaming deprecation, update SDK snippets for Python, Go, and Node, and perform a link‑check.
- Jin Park volunteered to own the release‑notes copy and merge order; Marta Gomez to update SDK snippets; Kevin Liu to run the link‑check and submit fixes.

#eng-runtime: 5832109999

Priya Nair: quick sync — tradeoffs between per-tenant private queues vs a single shared backlog for continuous batching. I sketched two proposals and want quick reactions: 1) explicit per-tenant queue objects with quotas/headroom; 2) shared backlog with budget hints to admission. thoughts?

Ethan Cole: +1 to evaluating budget hints. Per-tenant queues feel simpler for isolation but explode resource tracking.

Samir Gupta: my worry with per-tenant queues is long-tail memory blowup when many idle tenants have pinned structures. We saw this in loadgen. Shared backlog + soft budgets gives better headroom packing.

Marta Lopez: can we combine? ephemeral per-tenant handles that are created on demand and retired after idle timeout. keep a compact index into shared backlog.

Ava Chen: agreement on ephemeral handles — cheap metadata, ownership lease. also need clear interface between admission and coalescer. admission should return a small opaque token, not raw queue pointer.

Priya Nair: here's the budget-hint API I prototyped:
```json
{
  "budget_hint": {
    "latency_target_ms": 50,
    "cost_priority": "low|medium|high",
    "max_tokens_estimate": 128
  }
}
```
Admission uses it for placement/credits; coalescer can use only latency_target_ms when shaping windows.

Ethan Cole: nice. we should also expose a boolean `prefetch_ok` for i/o-heavy prompts so admission can favor warm kvcache lanes.

Samir Gupta: semantics question: are budget hints soft or authoritative? i.e., do we treat them as hints the scheduler may violate under pressure? I prefer soft hints with monitoring and regression alerts.

Marta Lopez: +1 soft. if we ever enforce hard budgets we'd need billing hooks. soft hints allow graceful degradation policies: spill to dedicated pool, lower quant, or fallback model.

Ava Chen: about the interface boundary: admission -> returns AdmissionTicket {lease_id, credits, estimated_window_ms}. coalescer consumes AdmissionTicket but should never mutate scheduler state. ticket revocation via heartbeat only. avoids tight coupling.

Priya Nair: good. That implies a small ticket lifecycle: claim -> coalesce -> execute -> release. We need APIs for early release and forced revoke on overload.

Ethan Cole: observability: emit token-level traces with budget_hint and ticket lifecycle events. want to be able to reconstruct why a request got a tiny vs big window.

Samir Gupta: on long-term refactor: split admission into two phases: fast-path cheap heuristics (latency SLOs) and slow-path optimizer (packing for throughput). both share same metadata store but different time budgets. This helps keeping p99 low.

Marta Lopez: implementation note: keep admission idempotent for retries. multi-step admission must be safe under duplicate requests and client-side retries.

Ava Chen: experiments to run: 1) shared backlog + ephemeral handles with budget hints; 2) per-tenant queues with aggressive retirement; measure p50/p95/p99 and memory tail. run in canary with synthetic tenant fanout.

Priya Nair: action items: @Ethan create experiment harness (1 week), @Samir add budget_hint fields to request proto and wire up telemetry, @Marta prototype ephemeral handle lifecycle, @Ava draft ticket lifecycle API. I'll coordinate rollout gating and regression alerts.

deploy-bot: canary cluster deploy queued: batcher-v2-experiment -> staging. ETA 14m. :rocket:

Ethan Cole: noted, will start harness and share slack with metrics link.

Samir Gupta: starting proto PR today, will tag the team.

Marta Lopez: will prototype retire worker by Friday.

Ava Chen: drafting API now, will push RFC doc to /eng-runtime.

Priya Nair: thanks all — keeping this tight. if we find simple wins (budget_hint + ticket) we may avoid huge refactor. if not, split admission phases is our fallback.

Actor: ['ben-carter']
Occurred at: 5567890450000
Content:
#product: 5567890123

Nina Park: Heads up 1: we have two regulated pilot customers asking for SLA exceptions (longer data residency commitments + legal indemnities). Need a quick alignment: what parts of GA gating do we relax vs what must stay for public GA? @Raj @Omar @Lena @Ben @Emma

Raj Singh: Short answer: legal can approve limited SLA exceptions for named accounts but only if security controls (VPC, KMS) and audit logs are in place. We need clear scope and a time-limited clause (e.g., 6 months) and commercial signoff. Also require change to TOS addendum.

Omar Reyes: Security checklist for exceptions: 1) Dedicated or Private deployment, 2) Customer-managed KMS, 3) Audit log retention >= 1 year, 4) Pen-test within 90 days, 5) Network allowlist. If any of these are missing, we cannot relax data residency.

Lena Mfcller: Sales note: both customers are willing to sign a pilot addendum and pay for extra support. They want SLA credits higher than standard (e.g., 99.99 for Dedicated). We should capture an explicit billing backstop if we agree to tighter SLA.

Ben Carter: Operationally we can support 99.99 for small committed Dedicated pools but it requires reserved capacity + on-call increase during pilot. We should model cost: estimate +30% on ops headcount for the pilot period. Also need a fallback policy if infra risk appears (auto-degrade to 99.95 and notify).

Emma Zhao: Finance: we'll need a billing backstop clause that limits credits (max X months) and ties credits to measured downtime only. Also need telemetry to validate $/token and effective cost delta for Dedicated vs Hosted. I can fast-track a simple P&L if Ben shares ops delta.

DeployBot: NOTE: security controls doc template: https://internal.docs/redwood/security-exception-template (created).

Nina Park: Proposed path: 1) Draft exception addendum (Raj) by Wed, 2) Security gap check (Omar + Ben) Tue, 3) Draft billing backstop terms (Emma) Wed, 4) Sales finalize customer addendum (Lena) Thu. Pilot starts only after checklist cleared. Any objections?

Raj Singh: No objections. Will include TOS change + 6-month sunset + liability caps.

Omar Reyes: Will run gap check Tue morning and upload findings to the shared doc.

Ben Carter: Will model ops cost and a fallback automation by Tue EOD.

Emma Zhao: Will prepare billing backstop language and a short financial model by Wed.

Lena Mfcller: Fine by me. I'll align customers and use the addendum template.

Nina Park: Great. Quick ask: if we accept these exceptions for pilot, do we mark feature as "GA with exceptions" on the product page or keep it as "Pilot/Private only" until we generalize? Thoughts?

Raj Singh: Keep public GA as-is. Exceptions should be private/pilot-only and documented in the addendum.

Ben Carter: Agreed. Also add explicit rollback triggers (incidents > X in 30 days -> reevaluate SLA).

Nina Park: Thanks all. I'll compile notes and update the pre-GA checklist with an "Exceptions" section. Will post updated checklist here once done. :white_check_mark:

The conversation covers documentation PR coordination and a product planning session for cerberus‑lite‑1 onboarding and falcon‑old‑v2 deprecation.

- Samira Khan said she would validate performance and memory numbers in the matrix and later updated the compatibility table with rounded numbers.
- Priya R. added a known‑issues note about dedupe cache increasing first‑token latency on cold starts for some quantized configurations.
- Noah suggested keeping link fixes and snippet changes as separate PRs to simplify rollbacks.
- Ava Chen agreed and planned to split the draft into three branches (notes‑only, sdk‑examples, link‑fixes) and asked Jin to coordinate merge order.

- Diego Ruiz asked about target customers for cerberus‑lite‑1 and requested compatibility checks for prompt‑schema differences with an automated smoke test.
- Priya Natarajan defined the primary use case for cerberus‑lite‑1 as latency‑sensitive mobile with cheap embedding fallback, classifying it as a “tier B” model with opt‑in pin, and suggested migration credit of 20 % of monthly token spend or $300/mo whichever is higher.

#product: 5220001111

Evan Park: Quick thread 99need alignment on two catalog items for next sprint: (1) 'cerberus-lite-1' onboarding as a fast-path small model, (2) staged deprecation timeline for 'falcon-old-v2' pinned customers. High level proposal in the next message.
Leah Morgan: +1 let's capture launch gates early.
Diego Ruiz: Curious about target customers for cerberus-lite-1. Is this for low-latency mobile use cases or just cost-sensitive tenants?
Priya Natarajan: Primary use case = latency-sensitive mobile + cheap embedding fallback. We should classify as "tier B" model with opt-in pin.
Ben Cho: Ops note: cerberus-lite-1 requires different quantization runtime. Need infra sign-off. Can reserve a 1-GPU test pool.
Evan Park: Proposal bullets:
- Cerberus-lite-1 onboarding: 7-day quick canary (hosted only), bench targets: p95 <= 90ms, regressions <=3% vs baseline on mobile prompts, token-cost <= -12% (cheaper). Gate: perf harness green + infra smoke.
- Falcon-old-v2 deprecation: 3 cohort rollout: internal -> partner opt-in -> pinned customers with 45d opt-out -> forced retire at 90d. Provide a migration credit program for impacted customers for 60 days.
Leah Morgan: Migration credit = how many free tokens? we should propose a $/month credit estimate rather than tokens. Customers understand dollars.
Priya Natarajan: Agree. Suggest credit = 20% of monthly estimated token spend or flat $300/mo whichever is higher, for customers on pinned contracts.
Diego Ruiz: We must run compatibility checks for prompt schema differences; add an automated smoke that compares top-10 customer prompt outputs and flags >7% semantic change.
Ben Cho: I'll spin up the 1-GPU pool and run a quick quant-tune. ETA: Tue morning.
Evan Park: Acceptance criteria for cerberus-lite-1 (short):
- perf harness: p95 <= 90ms
- quality pass >= 97% on eval_mobile_v2
- infra smoke: no OOMs at 95th percentile concurrent load
- docs: quickstart + known-diff section published
Leah Morgan: For deprecation comms: we need a staged email + console banner + in-product modal for pinned users. Drafting copy now.
Priya Natarajan: Legal check: migration credit formula in contract addendum. I'll ping legal.
Diego Ruiz: Can we add a "compat shim" flag to route to falcon-old-v2 fallback for 30 days post-migrate? That reduces customer risk.
Ben Cho: Technically feasible. Will need routing config and cost cap guardrails.
comm-bot: Ticket created: catalog-cerberus-lite-5220001111 (status: open), ticket: falcon-old-v2-deprec-5220001111 (status: drafting).
Evan Park: Decision asks by Wed EOD: (A) greenlight cerberus-lite-1 quick canary with the listed gates, (B) approve deprecation cohorts + migration credit formula. Please reply with approve/need-changes.
Leah Morgan: Approve (A) pending perf results. Need to iterate comm copy for (B) before final approve.
Priya Natarajan: Approve (B) in principle, pending legal on contract language.
Diego Ruiz: Approve (A) and (B) if compat shim routing is in place.
Ben Cho: Approve (A) once infra smoke completes. I'll report back Tue.
Evan Park: Thanks all 99I'll lock these into the sprint if no objections by Wed EOD.
